commands.label: Create BugDB Bug

examples: create a BugDB bug for HCM change validation
create bug with subject Payroll validation failure
create a new bug as duplicate/backport child of 39451267
parameter_hints: Use the provided subject or description as the BugDB subject through the repo-common bugdb_create_bug.py helper.
If base_rptno is supplied, preserve it as the base bug reference.
After creation, later catalog commands may use ${bug} for the created bug number.

instructions: BugDB bug creation is approved only when the description/subject is explicit.
The parent runner executes runner_common/bugdb_create_bug.py and captures the created bug number as the sequence variable ${bug}.
Downstream commands can use ${bug}, ${bug_ref}, or ${txn} after this command completes.

runner_execution.summary: Create a BugDB bug outside Codex through runner_common/bugdb_create_bug.py and capture the created bug number.

commands.label: Create ADE View
instructions: ADE view and transaction creation must happen outside the Codex child process. For explicit catalog Commands blocks and Codex CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND handoff lines, the parent runner executes the installed ADE Lens CreateViewAndTxn.sh helper.
Use the installed ADE Lens scripts/ade-scripts/CreateViewAndTxn.sh path. The older ~/.codex/skills/ade-lens/scripts/CreateViewAndTxn.sh path is obsolete in ADE Lens 1.0.7 and newer.
CreateViewAndTxn.sh requires -view <viewname> -txn <txnname> and optional -branch <series>; never call it with positional BUG/VIEW/TXN, VIEW/TXN/BUG, or VIEW/TXN forms.
Pass ADE label series names such as FUSIONAPPS_PT.V2MIBHCMBRONZE_LINUX.X64 with -branch. Do not pass /ade_autofs/...rdd paths to -branch.
The parent runner normalizes branch aliases and ADE path-like values before calling CreateViewAndTxn.sh; FUSIONAPPS_* aliases without _LINUX.X64 are expanded to the full ADE series name.

approval.guidance: Createview is a state-changing ADE lifecycle command. Confirm the target view, transaction, and branch first; after approval, hand off to the parent runner with CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.create-view view=<viewname> txn=<txnname> branch=<series>.

runner_execution.summary: Create ADE view and transaction outside Codex through installed ADE Lens CreateViewAndTxn.sh.

commands.label: Create ADE view and transaction outside Codex
argv:
set -e
helper_new="/scratch/rnerolu/.codex/skills/ade-lens/scripts/ade-scripts/CreateViewAndTxn.sh"
helper_old="/scratch/rnerolu/.codex/skills/ade-lens/scripts/CreateViewAndTxn.sh"
if [ -f "$helper_new" ]; then
  helper="$helper_new"
elif [ -f "$helper_old" ]; then
  helper="$helper_old"
else
  echo "Missing ADE Lens helper: $helper_new" >&2
  echo "Legacy helper path also missing: $helper_old" >&2
  exit 127
fi
echo "Runner diagnostic: using ADE Lens helper $helper"
args=("-view" "{{view}}" "-txn" "{{txn}}")
branch="{{branch}}"
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  branch="${branch%/}"
  case "$branch" in
    */LATEST) branch="${branch%/LATEST}" ;;
  esac
  branch="${branch##*/}"
  branch="${branch%.rdd}"
  case "$branch" in
    FUSIONAPPS_*)
      case "$branch" in
        *_LINUX.X64*) ;;
        *) branch="${branch}_LINUX.X64" ;;
      esac
      ;;
  esac
  echo "Runner diagnostic: using ADE branch $branch"
  args+=("-branch" "$branch")
fi
exec bash "$helper" "${args[@]}"

commands.label: Begin ADE Backport Transaction
instructions: ADE release backport transaction creation must happen outside the Codex child process.

Use this command after the backport bug has been created and the canonical release branch has been selected. For 26.07 the ADE base label series must be FUSIONAPPS_11.13.26.07.0_LINUX.X64, not a compact HWM-style series.

Before this handoff, verify the backport bug's BugDB setup by readback: Generic / Port Specific header field (GOPS, readback key gops) is I / Request For Inclusion; Bug Type (FusionApps) tracking group is Backport; status is 51; customer is INTERNAL -- FUSIONAPPS; and tags include CUSTOMER_IMPACT plus the release bundle tag for the target release, for example REL13BUNDLE-2607-PB8 for 26.07. If HCM bug validation names a different expected REL13BUNDLE tag, use that exact tag.
If ADE reports that the bug is neither a BLR, MLR, CI nor an RFI, do not retry this command unchanged; fix the BugDB GOPS value to I and verify the readback first.

The parent runner creates the ADE view if it is missing, captures the current view label, and runs ade begintrans -backport <backport_bug> -fromtrans <source_user>_bug-<base_bug> -bl <view_label> with any caller-supplied options such as -nocheckout.

Do not expect the new ADE backport transaction to be named <user>_bug-<bug>; ADE assigns the backport-format transaction name and should automatically fetch/use the source Bronze transaction. After begintrans, discover the actual active transaction from ade pwv/ade_info.vmd; if describetrans shows no backend branched elements and no checked-out files, run ade fetchtrans <source_user>_bug-<base_bug> -ade_merge -nogui from inside the open backport transaction before BVT or OraReview. Use the actual transaction name for settransproperty, save, and OraReview.

approval.guidance: Backport transaction creation is state-changing. Confirm the view, backport bug, source transaction, branch, BugDB GOPS=I / Request For Inclusion, Bug Type (FusionApps)=Backport, status=51, customer=INTERNAL -- FUSIONAPPS, CUSTOMER_IMPACT tag, and the target-release REL13BUNDLE tag first; after approval and readback, hand off with CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.begin-backport-transaction.

runner_execution.summary: Create or enter the ADE release view and begin the backport transaction outside Codex.

commands.label: Begin ADE backport transaction outside Codex
argv:
set -euo pipefail
view="{{view}}"
branch="{{branch}}"
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  branch="${branch%/}"
  case "$branch" in
    */LATEST) branch="${branch%/LATEST}" ;;
  esac
  branch="${branch##*/}"
  branch="${branch%.rdd}"
  case "$branch" in
    FUSIONAPPS_*)
      case "$branch" in
        *_LINUX.X64*) ;;
        *) branch="${branch}_LINUX.X64" ;;
      esac
      ;;
  esac
fi
if [ ! -d "/ade/$view" ]; then
  if [ -z "$branch" ]; then
    echo "ADE branch is required when creating missing view $view" >&2
    exit 2
  fi
  echo "Runner diagnostic: creating missing ADE view $view on $branch"
  ade showlabels -series "$branch" -latest
  ade createview -series "$branch" -latest "$view"
fi
exec bash /scratch/rnerolu/codeshare/projects/codex_automation/runner_common/scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh -view "$view" -- '
set -euo pipefail
bug="{{bug}}"
from_transaction="{{from_transaction}}"
options="{{options}}"
view_label="${ADE_VIEW_LABEL:-}"
if [ -z "$view_label" ] && [ -n "${ADE_VIEW_ROOT:-}" ] && [ -f "$ADE_VIEW_ROOT/ade_info.vmd" ]; then
  view_label=$(sed -n "s/^VIEW_LABEL[[:space:]=:]*//p" "$ADE_VIEW_ROOT/ade_info.vmd" | awk "NF {print \$1}" | tail -1)
fi
if [ -z "$view_label" ]; then
  view_label=$(ade pwv | sed -n "s/^VIEW_LABEL[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*//p" | awk "NF {print \$1}" | tail -1)
fi
if [ -z "$view_label" ]; then
  echo "Unable to determine ADE view label for backport transaction" >&2
  exit 2
fi
active_txn=""
if [ -n "${ADE_VIEW_ROOT:-}" ] && [ -f "$ADE_VIEW_ROOT/ade_info.vmd" ]; then
  active_txn=$(sed -n "s/^VIEW_\(TXN\|TRANS\)_NAME[[:space:]=:]*//p" "$ADE_VIEW_ROOT/ade_info.vmd" | awk "NF {print \$1}" | tail -1)
fi
if [ -n "$active_txn" ] && [ "$active_txn" != "NONE" ]; then
  echo "Runner diagnostic: ADE backport transaction is already active: $active_txn"
  ade pwv
  exit 0
fi
cd "$ADE_VIEW_ROOT"
cmd=(ade begintrans -backport "$bug" -fromtrans "$from_transaction" -bl "$view_label")
if [ -n "$options" ]; then
  # shellcheck disable=SC2206
  extra_options=($options)
  cmd+=("${extra_options[@]}")
fi
printf "Runner diagnostic: executing"
printf " %q" "${cmd[@]}"
printf "\n"
"${cmd[@]}"
ade pwv
'

commands.label: Refresh View

examples: refreshview to latest label in rnerolu_v_bug_39435490_bronze
parameter_hints: Extract exactly one ADE view name from the request or current resumed issue context.
If the view is missing or ambiguous, ask only for the view with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
After the requester has approved refreshview and the view is known, do not run refreshview inside Codex; emit CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.refresh-view view=<viewname>.

approval.guidance: Refreshview is a state-changing ADE lifecycle command. Confirm the target view first; after approval, hand off to the parent runner with CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.refresh-view view=<viewname>.

instructions: ADE refreshview execution must happen outside the Codex child process. Codex should interpret natural-language requests, verify the target view and approval, then emit a final standalone handoff line: CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.refresh-view view=<viewname>.

The parent runner executes this command through the repo-common scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh wrapper with the default lightweight FUSIONAPPS_SKIP_FMWC=TRUE path and writes per-view refresh logs under /scratch/<user>/codex-refreshview-logs/<view>/.
Use at least the standard ADE lifecycle timeout budget; refreshview commonly needs up to 10 minutes.
If refreshview fails with ade_info.vmd.free or another ADE lock, follow ADE persist lock guidance and do not remove ade_locks.wrd automatically.

runner_execution.summary: Run ADE refreshview outside Codex through the local RunInView wrapper.

commands.label: Clean View

parameter_hints: Extract exactly one ADE view name from the request or current resumed issue context.
If the view is missing or ambiguous, ask only for the view with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
After the requester has approved cleanview and the view is known, do not run cleanview inside Codex; emit CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.clean-view view=<viewname>.

approval.guidance: Cleanview is a state-changing ADE lifecycle command. Confirm the target view first; after approval, hand off to the parent runner with CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.clean-view view=<viewname>.

instructions: ADE cleanview execution must happen outside the Codex child process. Codex should interpret natural-language requests, verify the target view and approval, then emit a final standalone handoff line: CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.clean-view view=<viewname>.
The parent runner executes this command through the repo-common scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh wrapper with the default lightweight FUSIONAPPS_SKIP_FMWC=TRUE path.
Cleanview must be its own parent-runner command. Do not chain commands after cleanview in the same ADE session.
If PMC should run after cleanview, emit a separate later handoff line for CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: hcm.pmc bug=<bugid> view=<viewname>; the PMC parent command re-enters with --full-fmwc.

runner_execution.summary: Run ADE cleanview outside Codex through the local RunInView wrapper.

commands.label: Destroy ADE View

examples: verify the view has no open transaction then destroy view with force option
parameter_hints: Use the current issue or resumed session context to infer exactly one ADE view when the follow-up does not repeat it.
If no unique view can be identified, ask only for the view with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
Proceed only when the requester explicitly asked to destroy, remove, delete, or cleanup the view.

instructions: ADE view cleanup: the requested action is view cleanup, not MergeReq status readback.
Before destroyview, verify the view has no open transaction and no checked-out files with ADE commands.
Use `ade destroyview -force -aborttrans <view>` only for the explicitly requested view.
Do not manually remove view directories or ADE lock files.

commands.label: Create OraReview
instructions: Use the orareview skill for upload/update workflow and required preflight checks.

For a first OraReview upload, no existing review is expected. If orareview -x or orareview -t <txn> -x says no review exists, and the approved task is to create OraReview after verifying the ADE view, transaction, saved changes, and reviewers, hand off to the parent runner with CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: orareview.create view=<viewname> bug=<bugid> [reviewers=<emails>].

The parent runner uses explicit reviewers when supplied; otherwise it reads /scratch/<USER>/codeshare/ade_defaultreviewers.txt, then legacy /scratch/<USER>/codeshare/defaultreviewers.txt, and stops with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input if no reviewers are available.
The parent runner uses the installed ADE Lens scripts/ade-scripts/CreateOra.sh helper. The older ~/.codex/skills/ade-lens/scripts/CreateOra.sh path is obsolete in ADE Lens 1.0.7 and newer.
Use OraReview MCP tools for read-only review metadata, reviewer status, comments, and merge-check details when helpful.

parameters.name: transaction
parameters.label: ADE transaction
parameters.description: Optional context for backport OraReview handoffs; CreateOra.sh derives the transaction from the view and bug.

set -e
helper_new="/scratch/rnerolu/.codex/skills/ade-lens/scripts/ade-scripts/CreateOra.sh"

commands.label: HCM Bug Validation

parameter_hints: Extract exactly one ADE transaction name or bug-derived transaction reference from the request. For backports, use the actual ADE backport transaction discovered from ade pwv/ade_info.vmd, not <user>_bug-<backport_bug>.

HCM bug validation can run with validation_ref=<actual_transaction>. For ADE backports where the transaction name is assigned after begintrans, it can also run with view=<view> and transaction_from_view=true; the parent runner derives the active transaction from the view before running hcmbugvalidation.
If the request has no transaction/bug-derived validation target or more than one plausible target, ask for clarification with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.

postcondition.description: The PASS verdict must be a standalone word in the validation output.
postcondition.failure_guidance: Do not run subsequent commands. Summarize the useful validation output and wait for user verification or follow-up fix instructions.

followup_remediation.summary: Use the parent-runner BugDB validation-field command for Fix ETA and Platform/O/S repairs. Use BugDB MCP for other explicit BugDB updates when available; if MCP lacks field-update support or cannot resolve FusionApps tracking groups, use dev-bugdb-helper for approved field and transaction updates, then rerun the same HCM bug validation command.
instructions: For HCM bug validation errors that name missing Bug Fix ETA or an incorrect O/S / Platform (O/S), do not hand-write a BugDB update shell. Emit CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: bugdb.fix-hcm-validation-fields bug=<bugid> fixeta=<DD-MON-YYYY> product_line_id=<expected_os_id>. Omit a field only when validation did not request it. The parent runner builds the required full BugDB header payload, fails on BugDB error JSON, verifies readback, and only then should validation be rerun.
Prefer BugDB MCP for existing bug updates when the needed field-update tool exists.

If MCP cannot update existing bug fields, use $CODEX_HOME/skills/dev-bugdb-helper/scripts/run-bugdb.sh for approved field updates only; CODEX_HOME should be /scratch/<runner-user>/.codex, the wrapper is pinned to /bin/python3.11, and global python3 must not be changed.

Resolve friendly field labels such as Bug Fix ETA, Bug Fix By, Component Version, or Platform (O/S) to exact BugDB API keys before constructing a bug-update payload. For product_id 2419, use cached metadata at docs/bugdb_header_fields_2419.json; call header-fields only if the cache is missing or does not contain the requested label. Do not guess field keys.
Do not print OAuth tokens or client secrets.

For product_id 2419, Fix ETA maps to fixeta; write it in DD-MON-YYYY format such as 12-JUN-2026 because ISO date strings can fail with ORA-01861 even though readback displays ISO format. Platform (O/S) maps to product_line_id; when validation expects 226, write product_line_id=226 and verify BugDB readback shows os=226 before rerunning validation.
Bug Type (FusionApps) is a tracking group, not a bug header field, and dev-bugdb-helper bugsmart-closure only posts the closure template comment; it does not update tracking group data.

When using dev-bugdb-helper for field updates, use supported syntax only: bug-update <rptno> --payload-file <json> for header fields, bug-update-transactions <rptno> --payload-file <json> for transaction or tracking-group fields, or raw <METHOD> <path> with --data-json/--payload-json and no trailing bug id. The helper does not support --set or --tg flags; construct a JSON payload file from current readback plus only the approved overrides, then pass that file with --payload-file. For FusionApps tracking-group updates, put the tg array in that JSON payload, for example {"reassign_bug":"N","tg":[{"tg_id":2963,"tg_operation":"C","tg_new_value":"Backport"}]}. Never append an extra bug number after a raw path.

For a normal HCM defect/code-fix bug, set Bug Type (FusionApps) to Defect with dev-bugdb-helper bug-update-transactions, preserving required header fields and including reassign_bug=N plus tg=[{tg_id:2963,tg_operation:C,tg_new_value:Defect}]. Use Tracking, Fwd Port, or Backport only when the workflow explicitly calls for that type.

For an ADE backport validation customer mismatch, keep the backport bug customer as INTERNAL -- FUSIONAPPS and align the base bug customer to the backport bug customer when the requester has approved this cleanup.

For an ADE backport validation duplicate-release error caused by an earlier discarded backport bug, update only the discarded stale bug so it is no longer an active candidate for the target release, then rerun validation for the fresh transaction.

When HCM bug validation reports tag mismatch and says BVT is enabled for the product/label series, do not add PREMERGE_BVT_PASS, PREMERGE_FAST_TRACK, P3_ENV_ID, or other BVT evidence tags manually. Run /net/phoenix851379.appsdev.fusionappsdphx1.oraclevcn.com/scratch/share/tools/pmg_validate from inside the confirmed ADE view instead. Before invoking it, verify /scratch/tmp exists and is writable because the script writes logs there, and verify sqlplus works in the loaded ADE view; use the existing Oracle client fallback if sqlplus is missing or cannot load libraries. Capture pmg_validate output, accept PREMERGE_BVT_PASS or PREMERGE_FAST_TRACK only after the script/tool creates or validates that evidence, then rerun this hcm.bug-validation command.
If BugDB MCP add_tracking_group_data_to_bug returns Unknown tracking group name for Bug Type (FusionApps), use the bug-update-transactions path instead of asking for manual input.
Verify BugDB audit/readback after updates, including CHG: Fix ETA->... and CHG: Bug Type (FusionApps)->Defect when applicable.
After applying explicit BugDB follow-up instructions, rerun validation and keep gating until exit code 0 plus standalone PASS.

runner_execution.summary: Run HCM bug validation outside Codex directly from the parent runner environment; no ADE view entry is required.

commands.label: Run HCM bug validation outside Codex
argv:
set -e
export COLUMNS=${COLUMNS:-160}
export LINES=${LINES:-40}
export TERM=${TERM:-xterm}
validation_ref="{{validation_ref}}"
view="{{view}}"
transaction_from_view="{{transaction_from_view}}"
case "$transaction_from_view" in
  true|True|1|yes|YES) derive_from_view=1 ;;
  *) derive_from_view=0 ;;
esac
if [ "$derive_from_view" = 1 ] && [ -n "$view" ]; then
  run_in_view="/scratch/rnerolu/codeshare/projects/codex_automation/runner_common/scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView"
  run_in_view="${run_in_view}.sh"
  pwv_output=$(bash "$run_in_view" -view "$view" -- "ade pwv")
  printf '%s\n' "$pwv_output"
  derived_ref=$(printf '%s\n' "$pwv_output" | awk -F: '/VIEW_TXN_NAME/ {gsub(/^[ \t]+|[ \t]+$/, "", $2); print $2; exit}')
  if [ -n "$derived_ref" ]; then
    validation_ref="$derived_ref"
  fi
elif [ -z "$validation_ref" ] && [ -n "$view" ]; then
  echo "Missing validation_ref. For backports provide view and transaction_from_view=true so the runner can derive the active transaction." >&2
  exit 2
fi
if [ -z "$validation_ref" ]; then
  echo "Missing validation_ref and unable to derive an active ADE transaction." >&2
  exit 2
fi
echo "Runner diagnostic: using HCM bug validation target ${validation_ref}"
output_file=$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/hcmbugvalidation.XXXXXX")
cleanup() { rm -f "$output_file"; }
trap cleanup EXIT
set +e
if [ "$validation_ref" = "{{validation_ref}}" ]; then
  if [ -n "${HCMDEV_ROOT:-}" ] && [ -x "$HCMDEV_ROOT/bin/hcmbugvalidation" ]; then
    "$HCMDEV_ROOT/bin/hcmbugvalidation" "{{validation_ref}}" >"$output_file" 2>&1
  else
    hcmbugvalidation "{{validation_ref}}" >"$output_file" 2>&1
  fi
else
  if [ -n "${HCMDEV_ROOT:-}" ] && [ -x "$HCMDEV_ROOT/bin/hcmbugvalidation" ]; then
    "$HCMDEV_ROOT/bin/hcmbugvalidation" "$validation_ref" >"$output_file" 2>&1
  else
    hcmbugvalidation "$validation_ref" >"$output_file" 2>&1
  fi
fi
validation_status=$?
set -e
cat "$output_file"
if grep -q "Bug is linked to an unrecognised bug product" "$output_file"; then
  product_id=$(sed -n 's/^[[:space:]]*\* Found value of: //p' "$output_file" | tail -1 | tr -d '[:space:]')
  case ",$product_id," in
    *,2418,*|*,2419,*|*,2422,*|*,2425,*|*,4472,*|*,5750,*|*,9520,*|*,5695,*|*,5510,*|*,5512,*)
      echo
      echo "Runner diagnostic: hcmbugvalidation reported BUGPRODWRONG for known HCM product ${product_id}."
      echo "Runner diagnostic: this usually means the runner session could not read HTOOLS2 hcm_bug_vp_map, not that the BugDB Product ID is wrong."
      echo "Runner diagnostic: HCMDEV_ROOT=${HCMDEV_ROOT:-}"
      echo "Runner diagnostic: useToolsTst=${useToolsTst:-} useScriptsSchema=${useScriptsSchema:-} useBugAu=${useBugAu:-}"
      if ! command -v sqlplus >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -x /usr/local/packages/oracleserver_remote/19.0.0/bin/sqlplus ]; then
        export ORACLE_HOME=/usr/local/packages/oracleserver_remote/19.0.0
        export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=${ORACLE_HOME}/lib${LD_LIBRARY_PATH:+:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH}}
        export PATH=${ORACLE_HOME}/bin:${PATH}
      fi
      if [ -n "${HCMDEV_ROOT:-}" ] && [ -f "$HCMDEV_ROOT/bin/checkerCommonProcs.sh" ] && command -v sqlplus >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        set +e
        # checkerCommonProcs/getCredential is the same credential path hcmbugvalidation uses for hcmdit/hcmtools.
        . "$HCMDEV_ROOT/bin/checkerCommonProcs.sh"
        htools_pw=$(getCredential hcmdit hcmtools 1 2>/dev/null)
        cred_status=$?
        if [ $cred_status -eq 0 ] && [ -n "$htools_pw" ]; then
          htools_counts=$(printf '%s\n' \
            'set heading off feedback off pages 0 lines 200 trimspool on' \
            'whenever sqlerror exit failure rollback' \
            'select count(*) from hcm_bug_vp_map;' \
            "select count(*) from hcm_bug_vp_map where product_id = ${product_id};" | \
            sqlplus -s -L "hcmtools/${htools_pw}@//phoenix56589.ad2.fusionappsdphx1.oraclevcn.com:1521/pdbhtools.ad2.fusionappsdphx1.oraclevcn.com" 2>&1)
          htools_status=$?
          echo "Runner diagnostic: HTOOLS2 hcm_bug_vp_map query exit=${htools_status}."
          echo "Runner diagnostic: HTOOLS2 hcm_bug_vp_map counts:"
          printf '%s\n' "$htools_counts" | sed 's/^/  /'
        else
          echo "Runner diagnostic: unable to retrieve hcmdit/hcmtools credential for HTOOLS2 product-map check."
        fi
        set -e
      else
        echo "Runner diagnostic: skipped HTOOLS2 product-map check because sqlplus or checkerCommonProcs.sh is unavailable."
      fi
      ;;
  esac
fi
exit $validation_status

guidance: If BUGPRODWRONG is reported for a known HCM product, treat it as a possible HTOOLS2 hcm_bug_vp_map visibility problem in the runner session; use the appended Runner diagnostic before asking for BugDB product changes.

commands.label: Fix HCM Validation BugDB Fields
instructions: Use this parent-runner command when HCM bug validation reports missing Bug Fix ETA or an incorrect O/S / Platform (O/S) value.

Do not hand-write dev-bugdb-helper bug-update payloads for these validation-field repairs. This command reads the current bug, builds the full required BugDB header payload, detects BugDB error JSON, verifies readback, and exits nonzero if the update did not land.
Use fixeta in DD-MON-YYYY format, for example 24-JUN-2026. Use product_line_id for the expected O/S value reported by validation, for example 226 for Linux-x86-64.

runner_execution.summary: Update HCM validation BugDB fields through a full required-field payload and verify readback before validation is rerun.

commands.label: HCM BVT PMG Validate
instructions: Run this only when HCM bug validation reports tag mismatch and says BVT is enabled for the product/label series.
Do not add PREMERGE_BVT_PASS, PREMERGE_FAST_TRACK, P3_ENV_ID, ENV_ID, or other evidence tags manually.

Run the shared pmg_validate script from inside the confirmed ADE view using the normal lightweight RunInView path first, pass the bug number when prompted, verify /scratch/tmp is writable first, and verify sqlplus works or use the Oracle client fallback.

If pmg_validate reports the view/transaction is not up to date or the premerge environment has not picked up the transaction, run runner_common/scripts/ade-scripts/refreshview_loop.sh --once inside the same ADE view and retry pmg_validate in a bounded loop until it processes the transaction or the retry limit is reached. For the long-running BVT pickup watcher, run refreshview_loop.sh without --once. The helper writes per-view logs under /scratch/<user>/codex-refreshview-logs/<view>/. If ADE reports refreshview is already in progress, the helper waits until VIEW_REFRESH_STATE returns OK, then continues the label polling loop.
If pmg_validate asks whether to merge without PREMERGE BVT PASS certification, answer N. Do not register fast track from automation unless the user explicitly approves that bypass.
Only after pmg_validate completes without errors, update the BugDB bug status to 39 using dev-bugdb-helper set-status and then rerun hcm.bug-validation for the same transaction.
After this command succeeds, rerun hcm.bug-validation for the same transaction and proceed only if validation exits 0 with standalone PASS.

runner_execution.summary: Run BVT/premerge pmg_validate inside the confirmed ADE view.

commands.label: Run HCM BVT pmg_validate outside Codex
HCM PMC

parameter_hints: Extract exactly one numeric bug id, accepting either a bare number or rnerolu_bug-<number> reference.
Derive the transaction name as rnerolu_bug-<bugid> and use that transaction to identify the target ADE view.

Natural-language PMC requests such as run PMC for bug <bugid> are handled by Codex first; after the bug, view, and approval are clear, emit CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: hcm.pmc bug=<bugid> view=<viewname> so the parent runner executes the reusable PMC command outside Codex.
If no bug id, multiple bug ids, or multiple matching view/transaction candidates are found, ask for clarification with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.

preflight: Extract exactly one numeric bug id and derive transaction name rnerolu_bug-{{bug}}.
Identify the ADE view whose active or target transaction is rnerolu_bug-{{bug}} using ADE as the source of truth; if already inside a view, verify it with ade pwv first.
If no unique matching view and transaction can be identified, stop and ask the requester for the view or transaction choice with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
Before execution, summarize the identified view, transaction, and exact PMC command sequence that will run outside Codex.

approval.guidance: Codex should interpret natural-language PMC requests, confirm the target bug/view when needed, and hand off to the parent runner with CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: hcm.pmc bug=<bugid> view=<viewname>. If either value is missing or ambiguous, ask only for that value. Do not run pmc_wrapper from inside the Codex session.

instructions: Run only inside the confirmed ADE view with active transaction rnerolu_bug-{{bug}}.

PMC execution must happen outside the Codex child process. For explicit catalog Commands blocks and Codex CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND handoff lines, the parent runner executes runner_execution with the repo-common scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh wrapper.

If starting outside the view, use the repo-common scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh wrapper or equivalent ADE setup to execute commands in the confirmed view environment; do not use the deployed ADE Lens RunInView.sh for this runner workflow.

For pre-cleanview ADE lifecycle checks in a PMC workflow, including ade pwv, ade lsco, ade showlabels, ade refreshview, and ade cleanview, keep the wrapper default FUSIONAPPS_SKIP_FMWC=TRUE path; do not run a standalone --full-fmwc entry before refreshview.

Use a minimum timeout budget of 600 seconds for every ADE command wrapper invocation, including read-only ADE checks, refreshview, cleanview, bug validation, checkin/save, OraReview, and MergeReq-related ADE preflight commands. Do not wrap ADE commands with timeout 120; refreshview commonly needs up to 10 minutes, and PMC or SeedData-specific helpers may use longer budgets when documented.

For actual PMC setup/run commands through scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh, pass --full-fmwc in the ADE session that will run pmc_wrapper so FUSIONAPPS_SKIP_FMWC is not set; regular non-PMC ADE tasks should keep the wrapper default FUSIONAPPS_SKIP_FMWC=TRUE path.

If ade cleanview is part of the PMC setup, run any needed refreshview before cleanview, then after cleanview completes, exit out of that ADE view session and do not run any further commands in it; re-enter the same ADE view in a fresh --full-fmwc session before ade pwv, ade lsco, ade expand, or pmc_wrapper. That re-entered session is the PMC-intended ADE session.

When using scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh, cleanview must be its own invocation; include an explicit blocking re-entry command such as scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh --full-fmwc -view <view> -- "cd $AVR; exit" only after cleanview. After that re-entry completes, batch sequential post-cleanview commands in that same fresh local wrapper session, using inline output checks for required checkpoints; do not re-enter the view for every command.
Never chain commands after cleanview in the same quoted command, shell, or ADE session.

When using scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh for the expand commands, keep $AVR inside the command argument passed to the local wrapper so it expands inside the ADE view session; do not let the outer /bin/bash -lc shell expand $AVR.
Run the expand commands before pmc_wrapper, exactly in the listed order.

After each expand command, inspect output inside the same loaded ADE session and stop before pmc_wrapper if it contains does not exist, skipping, Nothing to expand, or a resolved target such as /fusionapps/... or /fainteg that indicates $AVR was lost; do not split expand commands into separate RunInView.sh invocations solely to inspect output.

Before pmc_wrapper, check whether sqlplus works. If command -v sqlplus fails or sqlplus cannot load the Oracle client, and /usr/local/packages/oracleserver_remote/19.0.0/bin/sqlplus plus lib exist, export ORACLE_HOME=/usr/local/packages/oracleserver_remote/19.0.0 and LD_LIBRARY_PATH=${ORACLE_HOME}/lib in the same loaded ADE shell before pmc_wrapper; prepend ${ORACLE_HOME}/bin to PATH if needed.
After pmc_wrapper finishes, verify the generated PMC HTML and premerge artifacts before reporting success.

Treat PMC_CODE_ISSUE, PMC_CODE_ERROR, missing fusionapps/premerge/premerge_result.json, or a Central PMC section that contains only startup/initial checks as blocking. A PMC Wrapper Summary or Central PMC Run Summary failure caused only by JUnits/JUnit tests is not a blocking PMC failure for MergeReq; record it separately as expected JUnit risk and continue if all non-JUnit PMC blockers are clean.

If the PMC report shows BUG Validation Status: INVALID and no other PMC blocker, hand off manual HCM bug validation to the parent runner with CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: hcm.bug-validation validation_ref=<actual_transaction>. For a normal non-backport bug this can be the bug-derived transaction; for a backport use the actual ADE backport transaction name. Bug validation does not need an ADE view; require exit code 0 plus standalone PASS before continuing.

When manual bug validation PASSes, upload the parent stdout log or captured .txt output to BugDB with $CODEX_HOME/skills/dev-bugdb-helper/scripts/run-bugdb.sh attachment-upload {{bug}} <txt-file>, verify the attachment, and proceed; if manual bug validation is non-PASS, stop with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
If any non-JUnit blocking PMC marker is present, do not report PMC success and do not proceed to mergereq; include CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input with the exact report path and blocker.
Stop on the first failing command, report the command, exit code, and useful error text, and include CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
command_sequence: if ! command -v sqlplus >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -x /usr/local/packages/oracleserver_remote/19.0.0/bin/sqlplus ]; then export ORACLE_HOME=/usr/local/packages/oracleserver_remote/19.0.0; export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=${ORACLE_HOME}/lib; export PATH=${ORACLE_HOME}/bin:${PATH}; fi

commands.label: Run HCM PMC outside Codex

commands.label: Submit Merge Request
commands.template: mergereq submit view={{view}} bug={{bug}} mode={{mode}} manager={{manager}} release={{release}} cc={{cc}}

examples: refreshview to latest label and submit mergereq
parameter_hints: Use the current issue or resumed session context to infer the ADE view, active transaction, and bug when the follow-up does not repeat them.
Default missing mode to normal, default missing manager to alok.x.sahay@oracle.com, omit release when not supplied, and omit CC when not supplied.
If the requester asks to refreshview to latest label and submit MergeReq, include refreshview before submit in the proposed sequence.

If the same issue or resumed session already verified clean PMC/premerge evidence and uploaded PASS HCM bug-validation evidence for the unchanged transaction, do not include a separate repeat-evidence verification step.

instructions: Use the mergereq skill for submission workflow, property checks, and command generation.
Use Mergereq MCP tools for report-card details and current-transaction quality previews before submitting when available.

For HCM/ADE MergeReq submit or resend, default missing mode to normal email submit, default missing manager to alok.x.sahay@oracle.com, omit release unless explicitly provided or required by mergereq, and omit CC unless explicitly provided. Do not ask for these values when they are omitted. Parent-runner submission must use the exact catalog handoff CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: mergereq.submit view=<viewname> bug=<bugid> mode=normal manager=alok.x.sahay@oracle.com; never invent alternate command IDs such as hcm.mergereq.
For mode=normal, generate the normal email-submit MergeReq command, not check-only and not db-only/no-mail.

Before submitting or resending mergereq for HCM/ADE work, verify the latest PMC evidence for the active transaction unless the same issue or resumed session already verified clean PMC/premerge evidence and uploaded PASS HCM bug-validation evidence for this unchanged transaction.

When that evidence is already verified and the transaction has not changed, do not add a separate repeat-evidence verification step before refreshview or MergeReq submit; perform only minimal read-only ADE state checks needed to execute the approved sequence. When only checking existing PMC evidence before MergeReq, use lightweight read-only/ADE lifecycle checks; do not perform a full-FMWC re-entry unless rerunning PMC or entering the post-cleanview PMC setup session is actually required.
If the requester asks to refreshview to the latest label and submit MergeReq, include refreshview before submit after the required approval is present.

Do not submit mergereq when the latest PMC report has PMC_CODE_ISSUE, PMC_CODE_ERROR, missing premerge_result.json, or otherwise incomplete Central PMC results. A PMC Wrapper Summary or Central PMC Run Summary failure caused only by JUnits/JUnit tests is not a blocking PMC failure for MergeReq; record the expected JUnit risk and proceed when the non-JUnit PMC evidence is clean. If the only PMC concern is BUG Validation Status: INVALID, require manual HCM bug validation to PASS and upload the txt evidence to the bug first; report unresolved blockers with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input instead.

runner_execution.summary: Submit MergeReq outside Codex from the confirmed ADE view using the parent runner.

commands.label: Submit MergeReq outside Codex
argv:
set -e
export COLUMNS=${COLUMNS:-160}
export LINES=${LINES:-40}
export TERM=${TERM:-xterm}
mode="{{mode}}"
bug="{{bug}}"
manager="{{manager}}"
release="{{release}}"
cc="{{cc}}"
args=(mergereq)
case "$mode" in
  normal|"")
    args+=(--yes-mail --bug "$bug" --aru_automate --remove --resend)
    ;;
  check)
    args+=(--check --bug "$bug" --remove --resend)
    ;;
  db-only)
    args+=(--db-only --bug "$bug" --remove --resend)
    ;;
  no-mail)
    args+=(--no-mail --bug "$bug" --remove --resend)
    ;;
  *)
    echo "Unsupported MergeReq mode: $mode" >&2
    exit 2
    ;;
esac
if [ -n "$manager" ]; then args+=(--manager "$manager"); fi
if [ -n "$release" ]; then args+=(--release "$release"); fi
if [ -n "$cc" ]; then args+=(--cc "$cc"); fi
printf 'Runner diagnostic: executing'
printf ' %q' "${args[@]}"
printf '\n'
exec "${args[@]}"

commands.label: Merge Approved ADE Transaction

examples: MergeReq is approved, proceed with merge
merge approved transaction after mergereq approval
approved mergereq can you proceed with merge
parameter_hints: Use the current issue or resumed session context to infer the ADE view and active transaction when the follow-up does not repeat them.
If the approved/move-ready MergeReq report card or target view cannot be verified, ask for the missing value with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
Refresh the view before merge only when the requester explicitly allowed it and ADE state indicates refresh is needed.
After approval and readiness verification, emit CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.merge-approved-transaction view=<viewname> transaction=<txnname> so the parent runner executes the merge outside Codex.

instructions: This intent is for post-approval merge only, not PMC and not MergeReq submit/resend.

Approved MergeReq merge: treat this as a post-approval ADE merge workflow. Verify the approved/move-ready report card and active view/transaction, refresh the view only if needed and allowed, then use `ade savetrans; ade beginmerge; ade mergetrans; ade endmerge`.

Post-approval ADE merge execution must happen outside the Codex child process. After approval and readiness verification, emit CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.merge-approved-transaction view=<viewname> transaction=<txnname> so the parent runner executes runner_execution.
Do not rerun PMC and do not submit, resend, or reroute MergeReq unless explicitly requested.
Run merge commands from the confirmed ADE view and stop on the first ADE error.

runner_execution.summary: Merge the approved ADE transaction outside Codex from the confirmed ADE view using the parent runner.

commands.label: Merge approved ADE transaction outside Codex
argv:
set -e
export COLUMNS=${COLUMNS:-160}
export LINES=${LINES:-40}
export TERM=${TERM:-xterm}
transaction="{{transaction}}"
ade pwv
if [ -n "$transaction" ]; then
  active_txn=""
  if [ -n "${ADE_VIEW_ROOT:-}" ] && [ -f "$ADE_VIEW_ROOT/ade_info.vmd" ]; then
    active_txn=$(sed -n 's/^VIEW_\(TXN\|TRANS\)_NAME[[:space:]=:]*//p' "$ADE_VIEW_ROOT/ade_info.vmd" | awk 'NF {print $1}' | tail -1)
  fi
  if [ -n "$active_txn" ] && [ "$active_txn" != "$transaction" ]; then
    echo "Runner diagnostic: active transaction mismatch: expected $transaction, found $active_txn" >&2
    exit 2
  fi
fi
ade savetrans
ade beginmerge
ade mergetrans
ade endmerge

commands.label: Set BugDB Status

parameter_hints: Extract exactly one target BugDB bug number from the request or issue title.
Extract the requested numeric status exactly as written, including phrases such as status 36.
For duplicate/base references, extract exactly one duplicate/base bug number as base_rptno and use status 36.
If the target bug is missing or ambiguous, ask only for that value with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
If the request is a BugDB duplicate close and the duplicate/base bug is clear, status 36 is implied even when the text does not repeat the status code. Duplicate closes must never use status 96.

instructions: BugDB status changes are cataloged state-changing work. When the target bug and numeric status are unambiguous, emit CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: bugdb.set-status bug=<bugid> status=<status> [base_rptno=<bugid>] instead of asking for a second approval or hand-writing CODEX_RUNNER_SHELL.
Preserve the numeric status exactly as requested. For duplicate closes, use status 36. Never substitute status 96 for a duplicate close.
Use BugDB MCP only for read-only preflight when useful. The parent runner performs the mutation through dev-bugdb-helper and verifies by BugDB readback.
Do not use query-audit as a required success gate for this command; audit/report endpoints can be slow and must not turn a successful status update plus readback into a failed runner result.

runner_execution.summary: Set BugDB status outside Codex through dev-bugdb-helper, preserving the requested numeric status and verifying by readback.

commands.label: Set BugDB status outside Codex
argv:
set -euo pipefail
BUGDB="/scratch/rnerolu/.codex/skills/dev-bugdb-helper/scripts/run-bugdb.sh"
BUG="{{bug}}"
STATUS="{{status}}"
BASE_RPTNO="{{base_rptno}}"
if [ -n "$BASE_RPTNO" ]; then
  if [ -z "$STATUS" ]; then
    STATUS=36
  elif [ "$STATUS" != "36" ]; then
    echo "Duplicate BugDB closes must use status 36; got status ${STATUS}." >&2
    exit 2
  fi
fi
if [ -z "$STATUS" ]; then
  echo "Missing BugDB status. Provide status=<number>, or provide base_rptno for duplicate close status 36." >&2
  exit 2
fi
if [ ! -x "$BUGDB" ]; then
  echo "Missing BugDB helper: $BUGDB" >&2
  exit 127
fi
echo "Preflight: reading target bug ${BUG}"
target_file=$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/bugdb-target.XXXXXX")
payload_file=$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/bugdb-payload.XXXXXX")
final_file=$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/bugdb-final.XXXXXX")
cleanup() { rm -f "$target_file" "$payload_file" "$final_file"; }
trap cleanup EXIT
"$BUGDB" --compact bug-get "$BUG" --size 1 | tee "$target_file"
if [ -n "$BASE_RPTNO" ]; then
  echo "Preflight: reading base bug ${BASE_RPTNO}"
  "$BUGDB" --compact bug-get "$BASE_RPTNO" --size 1
  /bin/python3.11 -c 'import json, pathlib, sys
item=(json.loads(pathlib.Path(sys.argv[1]).read_text()).get("items") or [{}])[0]
if not item:
    raise SystemExit("bug-get returned no target bug")
status=int(sys.argv[2])
base=sys.argv[3]
def first(*values, default=None):
    for value in values:
        if value is not None:
            return value
    return default
payload={
    "product_id": first(item.get("product_id")),
    "component": first(item.get("component")),
    "component_version": first(item.get("component_version"), default="0.0.0.0"),
    "status": status,
    "bug_type": first(item.get("bug_type")),
    "gops": first(item.get("gops"), default="G"),
    "release_status": first(item.get("rs_release_status"), default="D"),
    "database_version": first(item.get("database_version"), default="N/A"),
    "product_line_id": first(item.get("product_line_id"), item.get("os")),
    "os_version": first(item.get("os_version"), default="NO DATA"),
    "release": first(item.get("release"), default="$null"),
    "severity": first(item.get("severity")),
    "desirability": first(item.get("desirability"), default="$null"),
    "support_contact": first(item.get("support_contact")),
    "assignee": first(item.get("assignee")),
    "customer": first(item.get("customer"), default="INTERNAL"),
    "subject": first(item.get("subject")),
    "security_flag": first(item.get("security_flag"), default="N"),
    "mail_response_type": first(item.get("mail_response_type"), default="E"),
    "base_rptno": int(base),
}
print(json.dumps(payload, separators=(",", ":")))' "$target_file" "$STATUS" "$BASE_RPTNO" > "$payload_file"
  echo "Updating ${BUG} to requested status ${STATUS} with base_rptno=${BASE_RPTNO}"
  "$BUGDB" --compact bug-update "$BUG" --payload-file "$payload_file"
else
  echo "Updating ${BUG} to requested status ${STATUS}"
  "$BUGDB" --compact set-status "$BUG" "$STATUS"
fi
echo "Verification: reading updated bug ${BUG}"
"$BUGDB" --compact bug-get "$BUG" --size 1 | tee "$final_file"
/bin/python3.11 -c 'import json, pathlib, sys
item=(json.loads(pathlib.Path(sys.argv[1]).read_text()).get("items") or [{}])[0]
expected_status=int(sys.argv[2])
expected_base=sys.argv[3]
actual_status=item.get("status")
if actual_status != expected_status:
    raise SystemExit("BugDB readback status mismatch: expected %s, got %s" % (expected_status, actual_status))
if expected_base:
    actual_base=item.get("base_rptno")
    if str(actual_base) != str(expected_base):
        raise SystemExit("BugDB readback base_rptno mismatch: expected %s, got %s" % (expected_base, actual_base))
print("Verified BugDB readback: status=%s%s" % (actual_status, (" base_rptno=" + str(item.get("base_rptno"))) if expected_base else ""))' "$final_file" "$STATUS" "$BASE_RPTNO"

commands.label: Fill BugSmart Closure

examples: fill bug closure template for bug 39452875
complete bugclosure with functional and technical summaries
parameter_hints: Extract exactly one BugDB bug number.
Use the supplied functional summary, testing recommendation, and technical summary as the closure template fields.
If the closure text is missing, ask for the missing fields instead of inventing them.

instructions: BugSmart closure posts the Fusion HCM closure template comment through dev-bugdb-helper bugsmart-closure.
This command is deterministic only when the three closure text fields are supplied by the requester or by prior approved context.
The helper does not update Bug Type (FusionApps); handle tracking group updates separately when validation requires them.

runner_execution.summary: Post the Fusion HCM BugSmart closure template through dev-bugdb-helper.

commands.label: Post BugSmart closure outside Codex
argv:
set -euo pipefail
BUGDB="/scratch/rnerolu/.codex/skills/dev-bugdb-helper/scripts/run-bugdb.sh"
BUG="{{bug}}"
FUNCTIONAL_SUMMARY="{{functional_summary}}"
REPRO_TESTING_RECOMMENDATIONS="{{repro_testing_recommendations}}"
TECHNICAL_SUMMARY="{{technical_summary}}"
DESCRIPTION="{{description}}"
if [ -z "$FUNCTIONAL_SUMMARY" ] && [ -n "$DESCRIPTION" ]; then
  FUNCTIONAL_SUMMARY="$DESCRIPTION"
fi
if [ -z "$REPRO_TESTING_RECOMMENDATIONS" ]; then
  REPRO_TESTING_RECOMMENDATIONS="Run HCM bug validation and BVT/premerge PMG validation for this backport transaction."
fi
if [ -z "$TECHNICAL_SUMMARY" ]; then
  TECHNICAL_SUMMARY="$FUNCTIONAL_SUMMARY"
fi
if [ -z "$FUNCTIONAL_SUMMARY" ]; then
  echo "Missing BugSmart functional_summary or description." >&2
  exit 2
fi
exec "$BUGDB" --compact bugsmart-closure "$BUG" --functional-summary "$FUNCTIONAL_SUMMARY" --repro-testing-recommendations "$REPRO_TESTING_RECOMMENDATIONS" --technical-summary "$TECHNICAL_SUMMARY" --non-interactive --yes
